lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਰਾਖਿ ਲੇਹੁ ਆਪਨ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਮ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: naanak raakh lehu aapan kar karam |7|
lines.english: Nanak: uplift them, and redeem them, O Lord - show Your Mercy! ||7||
So many lifetimes are wasted in these ways.
You are our Lord and Master; to You, I offer this prayer.
This body and soul are all Your property.
In Your Grace, there are so many joys!

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਹਮ ਬਾਰਿਕ ਤੇਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: tum maat pitaa ham baarik tere |
lines.english: You are our mother and father; we are Your children.
the whole creation is strung on Your thread.
No one knows Your limits.
O Highest of the High, Most Generous God,
That which has come from You is under Your Command.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮਰੀ ਗਤਿ ਮਿਤਿ ਤੁਮ ਹੀ ਜਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: tumaree gat mit tum hee jaanee |
lines.english: You alone know Your state and extent.
Salok:
Nanak, Your slave, is forever a sacrifice. ||8||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਨਹਾਰੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਛੋਡਿ ਕੈ ਲਾਗਹਿ ਆਨ ਸੁਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: denahaar prabh chhodd kai laageh aan suaae |
lines.english: One who renounces God the Giver, and attaches himself to other affairs

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਹੂ ਨ ਸੀਝਈ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਪਤਿ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak kahoo na seejhee bin naavai pat jaae |1|
lines.english: - O Nanak, he shall never succeed. Without the Name, he shall lose his honor. ||1||
for the sake of one thing withheld, he forfeits his faith.
He obtains ten things, and puts them behind him;

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਭੀ ਨ ਦੇਇ ਦਸ ਭੀ ਹਿਰਿ ਲੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: ek bhee na dee das bhee hir lee |
lines.english: But what if that one thing were not given, and the ten were taken away?
Ashtapadee:
Our Lord and Master cannot be moved by force.
Then, what could the fool say or do?
Unto Him, bow forever in adoration.
all pleasures come to abide in his mind.
O Nanak, obtains all things. ||1||
One who abides by the Lord's Will,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕੈ ਮਨਿ ਲਾਗਾ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਮੀਠਾ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kai man laagaa prabh meetthaa |
lines.english: That one, unto whose mind God seems sweet
God the Banker gives endless capital to the mortal,
who eats, drinks and expends it with pleasure and joy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪੁਨੀ ਅਮਾਨ ਕਛੁ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਸਾਹੁ ਲੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: apunee amaan kachh bahur saahu lee |
lines.english: If some of this capital is later taken back by the Banker,
He himself destroys his own credibility,
the ignorant person shows his anger.
the Lord will make him happy four times over.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕੀ ਬਸਤੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਗੈ ਰਾਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis kee basat tis aagai raakhai |
lines.english: When one offers to the Lord, that which belongs to the Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਰਿ ਉਸ ਕਾ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੁ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: bahur us kaa bisvaas na hovai |
lines.english: and he shall not again be trusted.
and willingly abides by the Will of God's Order,
The many forms of attachment to Maya shall surely pass away
- know that they are transitory.
O Nanak, our Lord and Master is merciful forever. ||2||
Whatever is seen, shall pass away;
and when it passes away, they feel regret in their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਰਖ ਕੀ ਛਾਇਆ ਸਿਉ ਰੰਗੁ ਲਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: birakh kee chhaaeaa siau rang laavai |
lines.english: People fall in love with the shade of the tree,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਉ ਹਾਥਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਕੇਹ ॥
lines.translit: taa kau haath na aavai keh |
lines.english: nothing shall come into her hands in this way.
One who gives her love to a passing traveler
and yet, the blindest of the blind cling to it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: man har ke naam kee preet sukhadaaee |
lines.english: O mind, the love of the Name of the Lord bestows peace.
False are body, wealth, and all relations.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਨਾਨਕ ਆਪਿ ਲਏ ਲਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: kar kirapaa naanak aap le laaee |3|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord, in His Mercy, unites us with Himself. ||3||
False are sexual desire and wild anger.
False are ego, possessiveness and Maya.
False are power, youth, wealth and property.
False are chariots, elephants, horses and expensive clothes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਥਿਆ ਰੰਗ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਪੇਖਿ ਹਸਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: mithiaa rang sang maaeaa pekh hasataa |
lines.english: False is the love of gathering wealth, and reveling in the sight of it.
False are deception, emotional attachment and egotistical pride.
Only devotional worship is permanent, and the Sanctuary of the Holy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਪਿ ਜਪਿ ਜੀਵੈ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: naanak jap jap jeevai har ke charan |4|
lines.english: Nanak lives by meditating, meditating on the Lotus Feet of the Lord. ||4||
False are pride and self-conceit.
False are the ears which listen to the slander of others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਥਿਆ ਹਸਤ ਪਰ ਦਰਬ ਕਉ ਹਿਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: mithiaa hasat par darab kau hireh |
lines.english: False are the hands which steal the wealth of others.
